author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: 1 John IV. 12–16.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “If we love one another, God abideth in us, and His love will be perfected in us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In this know we that we abide in Him, and He in us, because He hath given us of His Spirit.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And we have seen and are witnesses that the Father sent the Son to be the Saviour ofthe world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God abideth in him, and he in God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God is love; and he that abideth in love abideth in God, and God abideth in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Love is a sweet word, but sweeter the deed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To be always speaking of it, is not in our power: for we have many things to do, and divers businesses draw us different ways, so that our tongue has not leisure to be always speaking of love: as indeed our tongue could have nothing better to do.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But though we may not always be speaking of it, we may always keep it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Just as it is with the Alleluia which we sing at this present time, are we always doing this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not one hour, I do not say for the whole space of it, do we sing Alleluia, but barely during a few moments of one hour, and then give ourselves to something else.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now Alleluia, as ye already know, means, Praise ye the Lord.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that praises God with his tongue, cannot be always doing this: he that by his life and conduct praises God, can be doing it always.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Works of mercy, affections of charity, sanctity of piety, incorruptness of chastity, modesty of sobriety, these things are always to be practiced: whether we are in public, or at home; whether before men, or in our chamber; whether speaking, or holding our peace; whether occupied upon something, or free from occupation: these are always to be kept, because all these virtues which I have named are within.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But who is sufficient to name them all?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There is as it were the army of an emperor seated within in thy mind.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For as an emperor by his army does what he will, so the Lord Jesus Christ, once beginning to dwell in our inner man, (i.e. in the mind through faith), uses these virtues as His ministers.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And by these virtues which cannot be seen with eyes, and yet when they are named are praised—and they would not be praised except they were loved, not loved except they were seen; and if not loved except seen, they are seen with another eye, that is, with the inward beholding of the heart—by these invisible virtues, the members are visibly put in motion: the feet to walk, but whither? whither they are moved by the good will which as a soldier serves the good emperor: the hands to work; but what? that which is bidden by charity which is inspired within by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The members then are seen when they are put in motion; He that orders them within is not seen: and who He is that orders them within is known almost alone to Him that orders, and to him who within is ordered.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, brethren, ye heard just now when the Gospel was read, at least if ye had for it the ear not only of the body but also of the heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What said it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Take heed that ye do not your righteousness before men, to be seen of them.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did He mean to say this, that whatever good things we do, we should hide them from the eyes of men, and fear to be seen?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou fearest spectators thou wilt not have imitators: thou oughtest therefore to be seen.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But thou must not do it to the end thou mayest be seen.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not there should be the end of thy joy, not there the goal of thy rejoicing, that thou shouldest account thyself to have gotten the whole fruit of thy good work, when thou art seen and praised.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is nothing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Despise thyself when thou art praised, let Him be praised in thee who worketh by thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore do not for thine own praise work the good thou doest: but to the praise of Him from whom thou hast the power to do good.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: From thy self thou hast the ill doing, from God thou hast the well doing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: On the other hand, see perverse men, how preposterous they are.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What they do well, they will needs ascribe to themselves; if they do ill, they will needs accuse God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Reverse this distorted and preposterous proceeding, which puts the thing, as one may say, head downwards, which makes that undermost which is uppermost, and that upwards which is downwards.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Dost thou want to make God undermost and thyself uppermost?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou goest headlong, not elevatest thyself; for He is always above.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What then? thou well, and God ill? nay rather, say this, if thou wouldest speak more truly, I ill, He well; and what I do well from Him is the well-doing: for from myself whatever I do is ill.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This confession strengthens the heart, and makes a firm foundation of love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if we ought to hide our good works lest they be seen of men, what becomes of that sentence of the Lord in the sermon which He delivered on the mount?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Where He said this, there He also said a little before, “Let your good works shine before men.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And He did not stop there, did not there make an end, but added, “And glorify your Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what saith the apostle?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And I was unknown by face unto the Churches of Judea which were in Christ: but they heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past, now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And in me they glorified God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See how he also, in regard that he became so widely known did not set the good in his own praise, but in the praise of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And as for him, in his own person, that he was one who laid waste the Church, a persecutor, envious, malignant, it is himself that confesses this, not we that reproach him therewith.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Paul loves to have his sins spoken of by us, that He may be glorified who healed such a disease.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For it was the hand of the Physician that cut and healed the greatness of the sore.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That voice from heaven prostrated the persecutor, and raised up the preacher; killed Saul, and quickened Paul.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For Saul was the persecutor of a holy man; thence had this man his name, when he persecuted the Christians: afterward of Saul he became Paul.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What does the name Paulus mean?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore when he was Saul, he was proud, lifted up; when he was Paul, he was lowly, little.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thus we say, I will see thee “paulo post,” i.e. after a little while.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear that he was made little: “For I am the least of the apostles; and, To me the least of all saints,” he saith in another place.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So was he among the apostles as the hem of the garment: but the Church of the Gentiles touched it, as did the woman which had the flux, and was made whole.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then, brethren, this I would say, this I do say, this if I might I would not leave unsaid: Let there be in you now these works, now those, according to the time, according to the hours, according to the days.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Are you always to be speaking? always to keep silence? always to be refreshing the body? always to be fasting? always to be giving bread to the needy? always to be clothing the naked? always to be visiting the sick? always to be bringing into agreement them that disagree? always to be burying the dead?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: No: but now this, now that.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These things are taken in hand, and they stop: but that which as emperor commands all the forces within neither hath beginning nor ought to stop.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let charity within have no intermission: let the offices of charity be exhibited according to the time.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let “brotherly love” then, as it is written, let “brotherly love continue.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But perchance it will have struck some of you all along, while we have been expounding to you this epistle of blessed John, why it is only “brotherly” love that he so emphatically commends.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “He that loveth his brother,” saith he: and, “a commandment is given us that we love one another.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Again and again it is of brotherly love that he speaks: but the love of God, i.e. the love with which we ought to love God, he has not so constantly named; howbeit, he has not altogether left it unspoken.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But concerning love of an enemy, almost throughout the epistle, he has said nothing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Although he vehemently preaches up and commends charity to us, he does not tell us to love our enemies, but tells us to love our brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But just now, when the Gospel was read, we heard, “For if ye love them that love you, what reward shall ye have?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Do not even the publicans this?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How is it then that John the apostle, as the thing of great concern to us in order to a certain perfection, commends brotherly love; whereas the Lord saith it is not enough that we love our brethren, but that we ought to extend that love so that we may reach even to enemies?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that reaches even unto enemies does not overleap the brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It must needs, like fire, first seize upon what is nearest, and so extend to what is further off.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A brother is nearer to thee than any chance person.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Again, that person has more hold upon thee whom thou knowest not, who yet is not against thee, than an enemy who is also against thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Extend thy love to them that are nearest, yet do not call this an extending: for it is almost loving thyself, to love them that are close to thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Extend it to the unknown, who have done thee no ill.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Pass even them: reach on to love thine enemies.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This at least the Lord commands.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why has the apostle here said nothing about loving an enemy.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All love, whether that which is called carnal, which is wont to be called not “dilectio” but “amor:” (for the word “dilectio” is wont to be used of better objects, and to be understood of better objects:) yet all love, dear brethren, hath in it a wishing well to those who are loved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For we ought not so to love, nor are we able so to love, (whether “diligere” or “amare:” for this latter word the Lord used when He said, “Petra, amas me?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Peter, lovest thou me?”) we ought not so to love men, as we hear gluttons say, I love thrushes.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou askest why he loves them?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That he may kill, that he may consume.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He says he loves, and to this end loves he them, that they may cease to be; to this end loves he them, that he may make away with them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And whatever we love in the way of food, to this end love we it, that it may be consumed and we recruited.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Are men to be so loved as to be consumed?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But there is a certain friendliness of well wishing, by which we desire at some time or other to do good to those whom we love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How if there be no good that we can do?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The benevolence, the wishing well, of itself sufficeth him that loves.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For we ought not to wish men to be wretched, that we may be enabled to practise works of mercy.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou givest bread to the hungry: but better it were that none hungered, and thou hadst none to give to.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou clothest the naked: oh that all were clothed, and this need existed not!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou buriest the dead: oh that it were come at last, that life where none shall die!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou reconcilest the quarrelling: oh that it were here at last, that eternal peace of Jerusalem, where none shall disagree!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For all these are offices done to necessities.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Take away the wretched; there will be an end to works of mercy.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The works of mercy will be at an end: shall the ardor of charity be quenched?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: With a truer touch of love thou lovest the happy man, to whom there is no good office thou canst do; purer will that love be, and far more unalloyed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if thou have done a kindness to the wretched, perchance thou desirest to lift up thyself over against him, and wishest him to be subject to thee, who hast done the kindness to him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He was in need, thou didst bestow; thou seemest to thyself greater because thou didst bestow, than he upon whom it was bestowed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wish him thine equal, that ye both may be under the One Lord, on whom nothing can be bestowed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For in this the proud soul has passed bounds, and, in a manner, become avaricious.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, “The root of all evils is avarice;” and again it is said, “The beginning of all sin is pride.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And we ask, it may be, how these two sentences agree: “The root of all evils is avarice;” and, “The beginning of all sin is pride.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If pride is the beginning of all sin, then is pride the root of all evils.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now certainly, “the root of all evils is avarice.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We find that in pride there is also avarice, (or grasping;) for man has passed bounds: and what is it to be avaricious to go beyond that which sufficeth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Adam fell by pride: “the beginning of all sin is pride,” saith it: did he fall by grasping?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What more grasping, than he whom God could not suffice?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In fact, my brethren, we read how man was made after the image and likeness of God: and what said God of him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And let him have power over the fishes of the sea, and over the fowl of the heaven, and over all cattle which move upon the earth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Said He, Have power over men?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Have power,” saith He: He hath given him natural power: “have power” over what? “over the fishes of the sea, the fowl of the heaven, and all moving things which move upon the earth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why is this power over these things a natural power?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because man hath the power from this; that he was made after the image of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And in what was he made after God’s image?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the intellect, in the mind, in the inner man; in that he understands truth, distinguishes between right and wrong, knows by whom he was made, is able to understand his Creator, to praise his Creator: he hath this intelligence, who hath prudence.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore when many by evil lusts wore out in themselves the image of God, and by perversity of their manners extinguished the very flame, so to say, of intelligence, the Scripture cried aloud to them, “Become not ye as the horse and mule which have no understanding.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That is to say, I have set thee above the horse and mule; thee, I made after mine image, I have given thee power over these.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because they have not the rational mind: but thou by the rational mind art capable of truth, understandest what is above thee: be subject to Him that is above thee, and beneath thee shall those things be over which thou was set.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But because by sin man deserted Him whom he ought to be under, he is made subject to the things which he ought to be above.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark what I say: God, man, beasts: to wit, above thee, God; beneath thee, the beasts.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Acknowledge Him that is above thee, that those that are beneath thee may acknowledge thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thus, because Daniel acknowledged God above him, the lions acknowledged him above them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But if thou acknowledge not Him that is above thee, thou despisest thy superior, thou becomest subject to thine inferior.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Accordingly, how was the pride of the Egyptians quelled?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By the means of frogs and flies.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God might have sent lions: but a great man may be scared by a lion.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The prouder they were, the more by the means of things contemptible and feeble was their wicked neck broken.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But Daniel, lions acknowledge, because he was subject to God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What the martyrs who were cast to the wild beasts to fight with them, and were torn by the teeth of savage creatures, were they not under God? or were those three men servants of God, and the Maccabees not servants of God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The fire acknowledged as God’s servants the three men, whom it burned not, neither hurt their garments; and did it not acknowledge the Maccabees?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It acknowledged the Maccabees; it did, my brethren, acknowledge them also.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But there was need of a scourge, by the Lord’s permission: He hath said in Scripture, “He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For think ye, my brethren, the iron would have pierced into the vitals of the Lord unless He had permitted it, or that He would have hung fastened to the tree, unless it had been His will?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did not His own creature acknowledge Him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Or did He set an ensample of patience to His faithful ones?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see then, God delivered some visibly, some He delivered not visibly: yet all He spiritually delivered, spiritually deserted none.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Visibly He seemed to have deserted some, some He seemed to have rescued.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore rescued He some, that thou mayest not think that He had not power to rescue.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He has given proof that He has the power, to the end that where he doth it not, thou mayest understand a more secret will, not surmise difficulty of doing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what, brethren?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When we shall have come out of all these snares of mortality, when the times of temptation shall have passed away, when the river of this world shall have fleeted by, and we shall have received again that “first robe,” that immortality which by sinning we have lost, “when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption,” that is, this flesh shall have put on incorruption, “and this mortal shall have put on immortality;” the now perfected sons of God, in whom is no more need to be tempted, neither to be scourged, shall all creatures acknowledge: subjected to us shall all things be, if we here be subjected to God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So then ought the Christian to be, that he glory not over other “men.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For God hath given it thee to be over the beasts, i.e. to be better than the beasts.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This hast thou by nature; thou shalt always be better than a beast.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou wish to be better than another man, thou wilt begrudge him when thou shalt see him to be thine equal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to wish all men to be thine equals; and if by wisdom thou surpass any, thou oughtest to wish that he also may be wise.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As long as he is slow, he learns from thee; as long as he is untaught, he hath need of thee; and thou art seen to be the teacher, he the learner; therefore thou seemest to be the superior, because thou art the teacher; he the inferior, because the learner.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Except thou wish him thine equal, thou wishest to have him always a learner.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But if thou wish to have him always a learner, thou wilt be an envious teacher.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If an envious teacher, how wilt thou be a teacher?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I pray thee, do not teach him thine enviousness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle speaking of the bowels of charity: “I would that all were even as I.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In what sense did he wish all to be his equals?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In this was he superior to all, that by charity he wished all to be his equals.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I say then, man has past bounds; he would needs be greedy of more than his due, would be above men, he that was made above the beasts: and this is pride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And see what great works pride does.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lay it up in your hearts, how much alike, how much as it were upon a par, are the works it doeth, and the works of charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Charity feeds the hungry, and so does pride: charity, that God may be praised; pride, that itself may be praised.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Charity clothes the naked, so does pride: charity fasts, so does pride: charity buries the dead, so does pride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All good works which charity wishes to do, and does; pride, on the other hand, drives at the same, and, so to say, keeps her horses up to the mark.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But charity is between her and it, and leaves not place for ill-driven pride; not ill-driving, but ill-driven.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Woe to the man whose charioteer is pride, for he must needs go headlong!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But that, in the good that is done, it may not be pride that sets us on, who knows? who sees it? where is it? the works we see: mercy feeds, pride also feeds; mercy takes in the stranger, pride also takes in the stranger; mercy intercedes for the poor, pride also intercedes.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How is this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the works we see no difference.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I dare to say somewhat, but not I; Paul hath said it: charity dies, that is, a man having charity confesses the name of Christ, suffers martyrdom: pride also confesses, suffers also martyrdom.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The one hath charity, the other hath not charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But let him that hath not charity hear from the apostle: “If I distribute all my goods to the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So then the divine Scripture calls us off from the display of the face outwardly to that which is within; from this surface which is vaunted before men, it calls us off to that which is within.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Return to thy own conscience, question it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Do not consider what blossoms outwardly, but what root there is in the ground.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is lust rooted there?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A show there may be of good deeds, truly good works there cannot be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is charity rooted there?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Have no fear: nothing evil can come of that.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The proud caresses, love is severe.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The one clothes, the other smites.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the one clothes in order to please men, the other smites in order to correct by discipline.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: More accepted is the blow of charity than the alms of pride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Come then within, brethren; and in all things, whatsoever ye do, look unto God your witness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See, if He seeth, with what mind ye do it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If your heart accuse you not that ye do it for the sake of display, it is well: fear ye not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But when ye do good, fear not lest another see you.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Fear thou lest thou do it to the end that thou mayest be praised: let the other see it, that God may be praised.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if thou hidest it from the eyes of man, thou hidest it from the imitation of man, thou withdrawest from God His praise.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Two are there to whom thou doest the alms: two hunger; one for bread, the other for righteousness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Between these two famishing souls:—as it is written, “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled:”—between these two famishing persons thou the doer of the good work art set; if charity does the work by occasion of the one, therein it hath pity on both, it would succor both.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the one craves what he may eat, the other craves what he may imitate.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou feedest the one, give thyself as a pattern to the other; so hast thou given alms to both: the one thou hast caused to thank thee for killing his hunger, the other thou hast made to imitate thee by setting him an example.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Shew mercy then, as men of merciful hearts; because in loving enemies also, ye love brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Think not that John has given no precept concerning love of our enemy, because he has not ceased to speak of brotherly love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye love brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “How,” sayest thou, “do we love brethren?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I ask wherefore thou lovest an enemy.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore dost thou love him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That he may be whole in this life? what if it be not expedient for him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That he may be rich? what if by his very riches he shall be blinded?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That he may marry a wife? what if he shall have a bitter life of it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That he may have children? what if they shall be bad?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Uncertain therefore are these things which thou seemest to wish for thine enemy, in that thou lovest him; they are uncertain.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wish for him that he may have with thee eternal life; wish for him that he may be thy brother: when thou lovest him, thou lovest a brother.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For thou lovest in him not what he is, but what thou wishest that he may be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I once said to you, my beloved, if I mistake not: There is a log of timber lying in sight; a good workman has seen the log, not yet planed, just as it was hewn from the forest, he has taken a liking to it, he would make something out of it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For indeed he did not love it to this end that it should always remain thus.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In his art he has seen what it shall be, not in his liking what it is; and his liking is for the thing he will make of it, not for the thing it is.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So God loved us sinners.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We say that God loved sinners: for He saith, “They that are whole need not the Physician, but they that are sick.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did He love us sinners to the end we should still remain sinners?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As timber from the wood our Carpenter saw us, and had in His thoughts the building He would make thereof, not the unwrought timber that it was.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So too thou seest thine enemy striving against thee, raging, biting with words, exasperating with contumelies, harassing with hatred: thou hast regard to this in him, that he is a man.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou seest all these things that are against thee, that they were done by man; and thou seest in him that he was made by God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now that he was made man, was God’s doing: but that he hates thee, is his doing; that he has ill-will at thee, is his doing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what sayest thou in thy mind?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lord, be merciful to him, forgive him his sins, strike terror into him, change him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou lovest not in him what he is, but what thou wishest him to be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Consequently, when thou lovest an enemy, thou lovest a brother.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore, perfect love is the loving an enemy: which perfect love is in brotherly love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And let no man say that John the apostle has admonished us somewhat less, and the Lord Christ somewhat more.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: John has admonished us to love the brethren; Christ has admonished us to love even enemies.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark to what end Christ hath bidden thee to love thine enemies.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That they may remain always enemies?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If He bade it for this end, that they should remain enemies, thou hatest, not lovest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark how He Himself loved, i.e. because He would not that they should be still the persecutors they were, He said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whom He willed to be forgiven, them He willed to be changed: whom He willed to be changed, of enemies He deigned to make brethren, and did in truth make them so.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He was killed, was buried, rose again, ascended into heaven: sent the Holy Ghost to His disciples: they began with boldness to preach His name, they did miracles in the name of Him that was crucified and slain: those slayers of the Lord saw them; and they who in rage had shed His blood, by believing drank it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These things have I said, brethren, and somewhat at length: yet because charity was to be more earnestly commended to you, beloved, in this way was it to be commended.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if there be no charity in you, we have said nothing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But if it be in you, we have as it were cast oil upon the flames.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And in whom it was not, perchance by words it hath been kindled.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In one; that which was there hath grown; in another, that hath begun to be, which was not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To this end therefore have we said these things, that ye be not slow to love your enemies.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Does any man rage against thee? he rages, pray thou; he hates, pity thou.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is the fever of his soul that hates thee: he will be whole, and will thank thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How do physicians love them that are sick?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it the sick that they love?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If they love them as sick, they wish them to be always sick.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To this end love they the sick; not that they should still be sick, but that from being sick they should be made whole.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And how much have they very often to suffer from the frenzied!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What contumelious language!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Very often they are even struck by them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He attacks the fever, forgives the man.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what shall I say, brethren? does he love his enemy?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nay, he hates his enemy, the disease; for it is this that he hates, and loves the man by whom he is struck: he hates the fever.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For by whom or by what is he struck? by the disease, by the sickness, by the fever.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He takes away that which strives against him, that there may remain that from which he shall have thanks.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thine enemy hate thee, and unjustly hate thee; know that the lust of the world reigns in him, therefore he hates thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou also hate him, thou on the other hand renderest evil for evil.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What does it, to render evil for evil?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I wept for one sick man who hated thee; now bewail I thee, if thou also hatest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But he attacks thy property; he takes from thee I know not what things which thou hast on earth: therefore hatest thou him, because he puts thee to straits on earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be not thou straitened, remove thee to heaven above; there shalt thou have thine heart where there is wide room, so that thou mayest not be straitened in the hope of life eternal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Consider what the things are that he takes from thee: not even them would he take from thee, but by permission of Him who “scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He, this same enemy of thine, is in a manner the instrument in the hands of God, by which thou mayest be healed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If God knows it to be good for thee that he should despoil thee, He permits him; if He knows it to be good for thee that thou shouldest receive blows, He permits him to smite thee: by the means of Him He careth for thee: wish thou that he may be made whole.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “No man hath seen God at any time.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: See, beloved: “If we love one another, God will dwell in us, and His love will be perfected in us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Begin to love; thou shalt be perfected.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hast thou begun to love?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God has begun to dwell in thee: love Him that has begun to dwell in thee, that by more perfect indwelling He may make thee perfect.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “In this we know that we dwell in Him and He in us, because He hath given us of His Spirit.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is well: thanks be to God!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We come to know that He dwelleth in us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And whence come we to know this very thing, to wit, that we do know that He dwelleth in us?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because John himself has said this: “Because He hath given us of His Spirit.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence know we that He hath given us of His Spirit?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This very thing, that He hath given thee of His Spirit, whence comest thou to know it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ask thine own bowels: if they are full of charity, thou hast the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence know we that by this thou knowest that the Spirit of God dwelleth in thee?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Spirit which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And we have seen, and are witnesses, that God hath sent His Son to be the Saviour of the world.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Set your minds at rest, ye that are sick: such a Physician is come, and do ye despair?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Great were the diseases, incurable were the wounds, desperate was the sickness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Dost thou note the greatness of thine ill, and not note the omnipotence of the Physician?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou art desperate, but He is omnipotent; Whose witnesses are these that first were healed, and that announce the Physician: yet even they are made whole in hope rather than in the reality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For so saith the apostle: “For by hope we are saved.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We have begun therefore to be made whole in faith: but our wholeness shall be perfected “when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is hope, not the reality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But he that rejoiceth in hope shall hold the reality also: whereas he that hath not the hope, shall not be able to attain unto the reality.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him and he in God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now we may say it in not many words; “Whosoever shall confess;” not in word but in deed, not with tongue but with the life.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For many confess in words, but in deeds deny: “And we have known and believed the love which God hath in us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And again, by what hast thou come to know this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Love is God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He hath already said it above, behold he saith it again.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Love could not be more exceedingly commended to thee than that it should be called God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Haply thou wast ready to despise a gift of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And dost thou despise God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Love is God: and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God dwelleth in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Each mutually inhabiteth the other; He that holdeth, and he that is holden.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou dwellest in God, but that thou mayest be holden: God inhabiteth thee, but that He may hold thee, lest thou fall.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lest haply thou imagine that thou becomest an house of God in such sort as thine house supports thy flesh: if the house in which thou art withdraw itself from under thee, thou fallest; but if thou withdraw thyself, God falleth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When thou forsakest Him, He is none the less; when thou hast returned unto Him, He is none the greater.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou art healed, on Him thou wilt bestow nothing; thou art made clean, thou art new-made, thou art set right: He is a medicine to the unhealthy, is a rule for the crooked, is light for the bedarkened, is an habitation for the deserted.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All therefore is conferred on thee: see thou imagine not that ought is conferred upon God by thy coming unto Him: no, not so much as a slave.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Shall God, forsooth, not have servants if thou like not, if all like not?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God needs not the servants, but the servants need God: therefore saith the Psalm, “I have said unto the Lord, thou art my God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He is the true Lord.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what saith it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “For of my goods Thou hast no need.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou needest the good thou hast by thy servant.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thy servant needeth the good he hath by thee, that thou mayest feed him; thou also needest the good thou hast by thy servant, that he may help thee.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou canst not draw water for thyself, canst not cook for thyself, canst not run before thy horse, canst not tend thy beast.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou seest that thou needest the good thou hast by thy servant, thou needest his attendance.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore thou art not a true lord, while thou hast need of an inferior.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He is the true Lord, who seeks nothing from us; and woe to us if we seek not Him!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He seeks nothing from us: yet He sought us, when we sought not Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: One sheep had strayed; He found it, He brought it back on His shoulders rejoicing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And was the sheep necessary for the Shepherd, and not rather the Shepherd necessary for the sheep?—The more I love to speak of charity, the less willing am I that this epistle should be finished.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: None is more ardent in the commending of charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nothing more sweet is preached to you, nothing more wholesome drunk by you: but only thus if by godly living ye confirm in you the gift of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be not ungrateful for His so great grace, who, though He had one Only Son, would not that He should be alone a Son; but, that He might have brethren, adopted unto Him those who should with Him possess life eternal.
